gomodel:run-figure2-007/tir1/molecular_f.snippet: tir-1 acts as a signaling adaptor linking upstream stimuli to the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 p38 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/nsy1/molecular_f.snippet: nsy-1 is the MAPKKK of the p38 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/sek1/molecular_f.snippet: sek-1 is the MAPKK that phosphorylates PMK-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/pmk1/molecular_f.snippet: pmk-1 is the p38 MAP kinase of the intestinal immune cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hsp60/molecular_.snippet: hsp-60 mitochondrial chaperonin, induced as a UPRmt output marker.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/atfs1/molecular_.snippet: atfs-1 is the bZIP transcription factor driving the UPRmt transcriptional program.
gomodel:run-figure2-007/atfs1/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-007/aak2/molecular_f.snippet: aak-2 is the catalytic subunit of the C. elegans AMPK energy sensor.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/daf16/molecular_.snippet: daf-16/FOXO transcriptional activator of stress-response genes.
gomodel:run-figure2-007/daf16/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription activator activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-007/atf7/molecular_f.snippet: atf-7 bZIP transcription factor, direct PMK-1 substrate driving antimicrobial genes.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/cebp1/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-007/zip2/molecular_f.snippet: zip-2 bZIP transcription factor driving the P. aeruginosa/ESRE response.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/nhr86/molecular_.snippet: nhr-86/HNF-4 nuclear hormone receptor acting as a DNA-binding transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/fshr1/molecular_.snippet: fshr-1 GPCR-type transmembrane receptor at the intestinal plasma membrane.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/tir1/part_of.snippet: tir-1 is required for antibacterial defense; upstream of the intestinal p38 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/nsy1/part_of.snippet: nsy-1 acts in the conserved p38 MAPK cascade in C. elegans innate immunity.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/sek1/part_of.snippet: sek-1 functions in the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 MAPK cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/pmk1/part_of.snippet: pmk-1 is the terminal p38 kinase of the intestinal MAPK cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hsp60/part_of.snippet: hsp-60 induction is a hallmark output of the UPRmt.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/atfs1/part_of.snippet: atfs-1 is the master transcription factor of the UPRmt.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hif1/part_of.snippet: hif-1 regulates RNA Pol II transcription (ESRE-driven genes in the figure).
gomodel:run-figure2-007/hif1/part_of.term_label: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure2-007/daf16/part_of.snippet: DAF-16/FOXO activates cellular stress response genes in the nucleus (figure).
gomodel:run-figure2-007/daf16/part_of.justification: The re-fetched daf-16 GO annotation set returned only molecular-function/binding terms (no BP). daf-16/FOXO is a canonical sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor, so its activity is part_of regulation of RNA Pol II transcription, matching the figure's DAF-16 -> cellular stress response genes arrow. No process-specific real annotation was available to copy; the figure shows the transcriptional role but not the specific BP CURIE.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/skn1/part_of.snippet: skn-1/Nrf2 mediates the oxidative stress response, driving cellular stress response genes.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hsf1/part_of.snippet: hsf-1 drives the heat-shock/cellular stress response gene program.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/atf7/part_of.snippet: atf-7, phosphorylated by PMK-1, drives antimicrobial defence gene expression.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/zip4/part_of.snippet: zip-4 functions in defense against Gram-negative bacteria; modulates ZIP-2.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/cebp1/part_of.snippet: cebp-1 (CEBP-1/2) regulates RNA Pol II transcription; modulates ZIP-2 in the figure.
gomodel:run-figure2-007/cebp1/part_of.term_label: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure2-007/zip2/part_of.snippet: zip-2 drives the antibacterial/ESRE transcriptional response to P. aeruginosa.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/nhr86/part_of.snippet: NHR-86/HNF-4 senses a phenazine pathogen metabolite to activate antimicrobial defence genes (Peterson et al. 2023, Immunity).

gomodel:run-figure2-007/fshr1/part_of.snippet: fshr-1 GPCR is required for intestinal defense against Gram-negative bacteria.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hsp60/occurs_in.snippet: hsp-60 localizes to mitochondria (figure draws it in the mitochondrion).

gomodel:run-figure2-007/atfs1/occurs_in.snippet: atfs-1 accumulates in the nucleus to drive UPRmt transcription.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hif1/occurs_in.snippet: hif-1 acts as a nuclear transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/atf7/occurs_in.snippet: atf-7 is a nuclear transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/nhr86/occurs_in.snippet: nhr-86 nuclear receptor localizes to the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/fshr1/occurs_in.snippet: fshr-1 is a plasma-membrane GPCR (figure: transmembrane coil spanning the lumen/cell boundary).

gomodel:run-figure2-007/daf16/occurs_in.snippet: DAF-16/FOXO purple box is drawn acting on gene boxes inside the nucleus oval; no nucleus CC annotation was returned for daf-16 to cite, so this localization is a raw figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/skn1/occurs_in.snippet: SKN-1/Nrf2 purple box is drawn driving cellular stress response genes inside the nucleus oval; no nucleus CC annotation was returned for skn-1, so this is a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hsf1/occurs_in.snippet: HSF-1 purple box is drawn acting on cellular stress response genes inside the nucleus oval; no nucleus CC annotation was returned for hsf-1, so this is a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/cebp1/occurs_in.snippet: CEBP-1/2 purple box drawn in the nuclear/transcription zone acting on ZIP-2; no nucleus CC annotation returned for cebp-1, so this is a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/tir1/causal/gomo.snippet: tir-1 product is causally upstream, positive effect, of nsy-1: pathogen effector-triggered aggregation of TIR-1 on lysosome-related organelles (the figure's 'LRO stress / pH increase' intermediate) activates the NSY-1 MAPKKK of the p38 cascade (Tse-Kang et al. 2024, Immunity). Combines the figure's TIR-1->LRO stress and LRO stress->NSY-1 arrows into one upstream-positive edge via the LRO-stress intermediate.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/nsy1/causal/gomo.snippet: nsy-1 (MAPKKK) directly positively regulates sek-1 (MAPKK): NSY-1 binds and phosphorylates SEK-1 in the C. elegans p38 cascade (Tanaka-Hino et al. 2002); SEK-1 carries MAPKKK-binding (GO:0031435) to NSY-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/sek1/causal/gomo.snippet: sek-1 (MAPKK) directly positively regulates pmk-1 (p38 MAPK): SEK-1 phosphorylates/activates PMK-1 in the conserved p38 pathway of C. elegans innate immunity (Kim et al. 2002, Science).

gomodel:run-figure2-007/pmk1/causal/gomo.snippet: pmk-1 (p38 MAPK) directly positively regulates atf-7: PMK-1 phosphorylates the transcription factor ATF-7, converting it from repressor to activator of antimicrobial genes (Shivers et al. 2010); ATF-7 has MAP kinase binding (GO:0051019) to PMK-1.

PMK-1 branch upward arrow to DAF-16/FOXO ('pmk-1 product causally upstream, positive effect, of daf-16'). Flagged uncertain in the curator intent (confidence 0.3, 'transcription link uncertain'); drawn arrow only, no specific regulatory citation found, so recorded as a figure read for the curator to verify.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/aak2/causal/gomo.snippet: AMPK (aak-2) arrow to HIF-1 ('aak-2 product causally upstream, positive effect, of hif-1'). Direction and end-type flagged uncertain in the curator intent (confidence 0.35); drawn arrow only with no specific citation, recorded as a figure read for verification.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/zip4/causal/gomo.snippet: ZIP-4 purple box solid arrow to ZIP-2 ('zip-4 product causally upstream, negative effect, of zip-2'). Drawn as a clear (negative) regulatory arrow in the figure (confidence 0.9); mechanism (heterodimerization vs transcriptional) not specified, no specific PMID located, so recorded as figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/cebp1/causal/gom.snippet: CEBP-1/2 purple box arrow to ZIP-2 ('cebp-1 product causally upstream, negative effect, of zip-2'). Drawn negative regulatory arrow (confidence 0.9); mechanism unspecified and no specific PMID found, recorded as figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/tir1/has_input/C.snippet: Pyocyanin (green hexagon in lumen) solid downward arrow to TIR-1/SARM1. P. aeruginosa redox toxin sensed by the intestinal cell; modeled as the upstream stimulus the TIR-1 sensing activity acts on (acts indirectly via LRO/redox stress, not a verified direct binder). ChEBI:28157 pyocyanin is a figure read, not tool-verified.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hif1/has_input/C.snippet: Pyoverdine siderophore -> +Fe3+ -> HIF-1 link. Iron mobilized by the P. aeruginosa siderophore pyoverdine; figure end-type ambiguous (arrow vs T-bar). SIGN UNCERTAIN: iron normally promotes HIF-1 prolyl-hydroxylation/degradation (would be inhibitory of HIF-1 accumulation), so this is recorded as the iron input the HIF-1 axis senses, NOT asserted as a direct activator. ChEBI:29034 iron(3+) is a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/aak2/has_input/C.snippet: mtROS text at the mitochondrion with an arrow to AMPK (aak-2). Mitochondrial ROS is the upstream redox signal sensed by AMPK; modeled as input to the AMPK activity (acts via redox-sensitive intermediates, not a classic direct ligand). Flagged uncertain (confidence 0.35). ChEBI:26523 reactive oxygen species is a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/hsf1/has_input/C.snippet: HSF-1 linked to a ROS white box; figure end-type AND direction uncertain (could be ROS input to HSF-1 or HSF-1 output). Recorded as the ROS signal associated with HSF-1 (input) pending verification, rather than asserting a regulatory output. Confidence 0.3. ChEBI:26523 reactive oxygen species is a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-007/nhr86/has_small_.snippet: PCN (phenazine pathogen metabolite, green hexagon in lumen) downward arrow to NHR-86/HNF-4. Peterson et al. 2023 (Immunity) show NHR-86 is a non-canonical pattern-recognition nuclear receptor that DIRECTLY binds a P. aeruginosa phenazine metabolite as a ligand to activate antimicrobial transcription -> modeled as has small molecule activator (RO:0012001). ChEBI:27101 phenazine represents the figure's 'PCN'; exact phenazine species not tool-verified (curator flag, confidence 0.35).
